Overview:

At Foot Locker, we are about more than just sneakers – we’re about community and creating growth opportunities. That is why our internship program is designed to help you lace up for success! We are inviting students to gain real\-life experience in the dynamic retail industry, contribute to impactful projects with international teams, and continue learning and building the skills that are necessary to kickstart their career. By joining Foot Locker, you will contribute to a culture driven by creativity and passion, and you will be supported by leaders that foster your growth!  

The EMEA eCommerce team consists of 15 international team members and 5 sub\-teams: Digital Content, Digital Merchandizing, Digital Operations, GEO team and Dropship/Marketplace teams. The team is overseeing all aspects of Foot Locker’s eCommerce business, from operations to campaigns to strategy. The Digital Merchandizing Intern will be reporting to the eCom Manager – Digital Merchandising. The role will be assisting the E\-commerce team in creating the most relevant online shopping experience, by ensuring products are displayed and categorized correctly.
Responsibilities:
* Help with product merchandizing on key product pages.
* Assist with daily site checks, ensuring products are displayed correctly and links are working.
* Support with product performance reporting of specific categories.
* Participate in projects aiming to increase cross\- and up\-selling.
* Support with campaign execution.


Qualifications:
* You must be a full\-time student enrolled in a Bachelor or Master program in Marketing, Business or related fields.
* You must be located in the Netherlands.
* Strong communication \& openness to learn
* Attention to detail
* Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)


Benefits:
* The internship lasts **6 months**, starting on **September 15th, 2026**
* It is a **32 hour/week** internship
* We offer a **hybrid**, flexible working model (\+/\- 2 office days)
* You will receive a **monthly internship allowance** of EUR 400 for bachelor's students, and EUR 600 for master's students
* You will be part of a casual sneaker culture and experience a collaborative and supportive team environment


Please note: This is a non\-thesis internship.

Are you looking for an internship at one of Europe's most exciting companies? Do you want to hop on our life\-long learning journey?

  

**Your Mission Should You Choose To Accept It**



You'll play a vital role in our Business Marketing team, sitting right at the intersection of product and marketing. Based within our Connect product portfolio, you'll help keep every ongoing initiative moving — tracking projects, coordinating requests, and making sure the right people have the right information at the right time. Our team works across co\-working, membership, and M\&E products, and no two weeks look the same: one day you're pulling together an agenda for our weekly alignment meeting, the next you're researching emerging trends or spotting a gap before it becomes a problem. Expect real ownership from day one, exposure to how a European hospitality brand actually runs its marketing engine, and plenty of room to shape how we work as you go. This is a genuine opportunity to build skills in marketing strategy, project coordination, stakeholder management, and marketing operations that carry well beyond this role.



**In Case You Do Not Know Who, We Are (ahem):**



Known for being the rule breakers in hospitality, TSH is a fun, creative and inspiring environment where everyone can work, stay, learn and play and most importantly – be themselves. As an employer we look, act and think like a hotel, but instead we offer a lot more. Think student accommodation, long and short stay options, gyms, talks, events, rooftop bar and eat \& drink escapes as well as community and coworking spaces. Located in The Netherlands, Germany, Austria, Italy, France, Spain, and the UK, with several new openings on the way, this hybrid hospitality concept may take root in the heart of Europe, but our plans are set for going global.



**What You'll Do**


* Provide research support to develop global strategies across the portfolio
* Track ongoing initiatives across our project tracker, flagging delays, gaps, or missing owners before they slow anyone down.
* Act as the go\-between for product owners and marketing, gathering their upcoming needs — campaigns, launches, enablement materials — and translating them into clear asks for the team.
* Sit in on marketing channel updates and pull out the intel that matters: roadmap changes, new messaging, shifting timelines.
* Support the coordination of live projects across co\-working, membership, and OpenUp, from launch checklists to content trackers.
* Help prepare clear, concise materials — decks, one\-pagers, and status updates — that get straight to the point.
* Contribute your own ideas to team discussions — we want your perspective, not just your admin.


**What You'll Have**


* You're studying towards or have recently completed a degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.
* You have some hands\-on experience with marketing or project coordination — an internship, a student project, or a part\-time r

**Job Description** **About the role**


Looking to put your chemistry background and analytical mindset into practice on real\-world commercial projects? We are currently seeking a curious and collaborative **Technical Marketing Intern** to help turn complex scientific data into market\-driven product insights. *This role focuses strictly on data interpretation, literature research, and commercial strategy, so no hands\-on bench testing is required!*


This role bridges technical evaluation and commercial application to support real\-world product development and innovation projects. As a Technical Marketing Intern, you will conduct desk research, review scientific literature, analyze lab data, and support technical documentation and product testing activities. In this position, you will help connect chemical product properties with application performance characteristics to serve customer needs. This internship is scheduled to start on the **1st of October** for a duration of **6 months**. The role requires a minimum commitment of **2 days per week on\-site** at our **European Headquarters** in **Oegstgeest** with the flexibility to work from home up to **3 days per week**.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Conduct desk research and review scientific literature to support innovation and product development projects.
* Analyze laboratory data, interpret findings, and assist in creating comprehensive technical documentation.
* Familiarize yourself with laboratory workflows and submit lab requests to support testing pipelines.
* Assist in product testing and Voice of Customer activities to help translate chemical properties into key performance characteristics.
* Collaborate occasionally with R\&D and commercial stakeholders on puzzle\-solving and real\-world product development initiatives.

  

**Additional Information** **What We Offer**


We believe in nurturing talent and providing a platform where your ideas matter. When you join us, you can look forward to:

* **Competitive Compensation:** A monthly allowance of €550 (based on full\-time hours).
* **Travel Coverage:** Commuting allowance (if applicable).
* **Professional Growth:** Access to specific training programs designed to sharpen your skills throughout your internship.
* **Global Culture:** A collaborative, international environment where you are given real responsibility and the space to explore your potential.

**Important Application Details**

* **Language:** We only consider applications submitted in English.
* **Enrollment:** You must be currently enrolled at a Dutch university for the entire duration of your internship.
* **Direct Applicants Only:** We do not accept unsolicited referrals or resumes. Please apply directly using your personal email address.

**Why join Avery Dennison**


We are a global materials science and digital identification solutions company with locations in over 50 countries, and approximately 35,000 employees worldwide. As a science and innovation company,

Can you make The Social Hub Delft a favorite city hotspot for events, lectures, movie nights, exhibits and parties? We're looking for someone that is the open door to something beautiful, a new friendship, a business collaboration or unforgettable experience. The Social Hub Connector Intern gives our building a purpose and creates stories and adventures with and for, all of our guests.


**In Case You Don't Know Who We Are:**



Known for being the rule breakers in hospitality, TSH is a fun, creative and inspiring environment where everyone can work, stay, learn and play and most importantly – be themselves. As an employer we look, act and think like a hotel, but instead we offer a lot more. Think student accommodation, long and short stay options, gyms, talks, events, rooftop bar and eat \& drink escapes as well as community and coworking spaces. Located in The Netherlands, Germany, Austria, Italy, France and Spain with several new openings on the way, this hybrid hospitality concept may take root in the heart of Europe, but our plans are set for going global.


**What You'll Do**


* You will actively connect our semester, co\-working, hotel and extended stay community
* You will help with the set\-up of ongoing program of events with and for our internal community
* You, along with the Connector, are the eyes and ears of our community and will be the to go contact person in terms of events \& community activations
* You will build and maintain relations with partners to fill our annual calendar with on\-trend/on\-topic recurring events that appeal to our community
* You will host and engage with our local student ambassadors on a regular basis
* You will create/collect \& deliver digital content based on the marketing briefings (formats include text, video and images)
* You will own and manage the local TSH social channels and operate them within the brand guidelines

**Who You Are**


* You're currently enrolled in a University or an MBO/HBO educational programme
* You have an outgoing and confident personality. You kill it in front of an audience
* You are the connector: you have a natural interest and love to be around and with people
* You're professional \& responsible enough to work independently in a fast\-paced, sometimes chaotic atmosphere
* You're super social, always flexible, incredibly motivated and never without a sense of humor

Overview:

At Foot Locker, we are about more than just sneakers – we’re about community and creating growth opportunities. That is why our internship program is designed to help you lace up for success! We are inviting students to gain real\-life experience in the dynamic retail industry, contribute to impactful projects with international teams, and continue learning and building the skills that are necessary to kickstart their career. By joining Foot Locker, you will contribute to a culture driven by creativity and passion, and you will be supported by leaders that foster your growth!


Reporting directly to the Director, Talent Management \& Employee Experience, the Talent Intern will collaborate with colleagues across the Talent team on a variety of projects. You will join a team consisting of one Talent Acquisition Manager, two Recruiters, two Learning \& Development Specialists, and one People Experience Specialist.  

Our team is responsible for shaping and delivering the Talent strategy, guiding the full employee journey—from attraction and onboarding to development, engagement, and retention. We strive to ensure that our employees feel connected, supported, and inspired throughout their time at Foot Locker by driving initiatives that enhance the employee experience, foster inclusion, and strengthen our culture across Stores, Distribution, and Corporate environments. As a Talent Intern, you will have the opportunity to learn about what the Talent strategy entails, how we measure the impact of our initiatives and you will contribute to different projects which help enhance the employee experience, attract and develop talent.
Responsibilities:
* Support employer branding activities by coordinating and preparing LinkedIn content in collaboration with Talent Acquisition and Employee Experience.
* Contribute to employer branding development by supporting content performance tracking, competitor analysis, and helping identify themes that strengthen how Foot Locker is positioned as an employer.
* Contribute to the creation and maintenance of learning materials to support ongoing training programs and development initiatives for our corporate and field team members.
* Assist in collecting and analyzing learning data to support training needs analysis and identify opportunities to enhance learning across the organization.


Qualifications:
* You must be a full\-time student enrolled in a Bachelor or Master program in Human Resources or something similar.
* You must be located in the Netherlands.

**Soft Skills:*** Communication, adaptability, creativity, attention to detail, open to learn.

**Technical Skills:*** Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint), Social Media platforms.

The role: What's in the Box  


You'll be joining our international Fleet team, working across the Netherlands, Belgium, and France to keep our vehicles running efficiently and our operations scaling smoothly.  


Your focus will be to dig into fleet performance data, spot bottlenecks, and help the team move closer to operational excellence. Along the way, you'll be turning scattered know\-how into clear, standardized SOPs that make onboarding faster and process consistency easier across regions. This is your chance to see how a fast\-growing logistics operation works.  


This is a paid internship designed to offer hands\-on experience in a fast\-paced, international environment. As an intern, you will have structured guidance to support your learning and development. We aim to create an environment where you can build practical skills, gain exposure to how HelloFresh operates, and learn through doing. The internship is suitable for students whose educational requirements allow them to complete an internship during the proposed period. During the application process, we will ask required questions regarding internship eligibility and university requirements (such as duration, documentation, or approval criteria). Please ensure you are able to provide this information as part of your application.  


What you'll do: The Recipe  


Document operational processes and standard operating procedures (SOPs) to improve onboarding, knowledge sharing, and consistency across regions  
Analyze fleet performance data to identify trends, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities  
Investigate process bottlenecks alongside teams to find root causes and drive operational excellence  
Build and maintain performance dashboards and reporting tools in Google Sheets, and support small automations that cut down manual work  
Contribute to ad\-hoc reporting that feeds both operational and strategic decision\-making  
Collaborate with Fleet Coordinators, OEM partners, and suppliers to understand challenges and shape practical solutions  
Partner with colleagues across the Netherlands, Belgium, and France to learn how local differences shape fleet management  


What you'll bring: The Ingredients  


Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Analytics, Engineering, Supply Chain, or a related field (open to other backgrounds if you bring the right skills)  
Comfortable working in Excel or Google Sheets; familiarity with SQL, Python, or Tableau is a plus but not required  
Genuine interest in data\-driven analysis and process improvement approaches (Lean, Six Sigma, Kaizen)  
Clear communicator who can work well with stakeholders across different styles and backgrounds  
Proactive and independent, comfortable taking ownership of tasks  
Fluent in English; Dutch is a plus  


Above all, we are looking for people who will make HelloFresh better. There are many different ways of developing skills, and we love diverse experiences! So even if you don't "tick all t

**Job Summary**

Are you a creative student with a passion for marketing communications and an interest in the food and beverage industry? Join our Innovation team and help bring IFF's exciting flavor innovations and technologies to life through engaging internal communications.
  

  

IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients and health \& biosciences, delivering sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products.
  

  

Taste: Crafting unique, technology\-enabled flavor designs that define the identity of food and beverage brands, shape trends in flavor creation, and fuel market success.
As a student placement within our Innovation team based in Hilversum, Netherlands, you will coordinate marketing and communication activities to raise internal awareness of IFF innovation and technologies with our commercial and technical teams in Europe, Africa and Middle East.


You will work closely with the Regional Innovation leader and Marketing functions. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to kickstart their marketing communications career, while experiencing many facets of the fascinating world of flavors for food and beverages.


**Where You’ll Make a Difference**

* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams, including flavor innovation, marketing and product development, to help in developing and delivering internal marketing communication materials on IFF flavor innovation and technologies.
* Create inspiring, eye\-catching internal marketing and communication materials within IFF brand guidelines, using IFF templates (posters, leaflets, videos, etc.)
* Coordinate the regional distribution of the developed marketing and communication materials.

**What Makes You the Right Fit**

* Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Marketing Communications, Marketing or Business Administration, or a related field (you need to be still a student till the end of the internship).
* Passion for Marketing and Communication in the world of food and beverages, with a strong desire to learn and grow in this field. A background in food science or chemistry is a plus.
* Highly organized with a keen eye for detail and layout and the ability to manage and coordinate multiple tasks and deadlines.
* Proactive, creative, self\-motivated, and able to work both independently and collaboratively in an international environment.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Word, Excel,) and basic graphic and video creation design skills as a plus.

**A creative approach and a genuine interest in food and drinks!**

This is a paid internship position with a duration of 6 months. The intern will report to the Regional Innovation Director Taste EAME and works closely with the Marketing team. We value creativity, innovation, and a passion for marketing and communication. Join our team to gain practical marketing communications experience while making great impact on our internal technical marketing efforts.

### **Job Title**

Internship: Data Strategy
### **Job Description**

**Start Date: As of September 2026**

**Duration: at least / 6 months**

**Type: Internship (non\-thesis)**

**Weekly Presence: 40h (minimum 32h)**

**Location: Amsterdam**

**Internship Title**

**Data Analyst Intern – Marketing Analytics**

**Your role:**

We’re looking for a curious and driven Data Analyst Intern to support our Marketing Analytics team. In this role, you will help analyze campaign performance, customer behavior, or funnel management data to generate insights that guide marketing decisions. You’ll work with datasets, build dashboards, and contribute to insights that supports stakeholders across marketing and commercial teams.


**Key Responsibilities**

* Collect, clean, and analyze marketing and customer data.
* Build and maintain dashboards and reports (e.g., in Power BI/Qlik).
* Support performance analysis and experiment measurement.
* Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in data.
* Collaborate with marketing and data engineering teams on ad\-hoc insights.

**You’re the right fit if:**

* Currently pursuing a bachelor’s/master’s degree in data, analytics, business, or a related field.
* Strong analytical mindset with basic knowledge of SQL, Python, or Excel.
* Interest in AI (any experience strongly preferable), marketing, analytics and data storytelling.
* Ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights.

**How we work together**

We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office\-based teams, this means working in\-person at least 3 days per week. Onsite roles require full\-time presence in the company’s facilities. Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.


**We offer you:**  

A non\-stop growth opportunity within a global multinational company. In addition, we also want to renumerate you well for the opportunity. This includes:


* Monthly full\-time allowance between €500 \- €700 gross, depending on your education level and if you need to relocate to The Netherlands. Part\-time internships receive the allowance pro\-rata.
* Housing compensation. Should you need to relocate to near our office, we’ll offer you a monthly allowance of €300,\- net if location is in the vicinity of Amsterdam, €255,\- net for all other Philips locations. To be eligible, we’ll just ask for the rental contract from your landlord, and your usual home\-work travel distance needs to be more than 50 km or 1\.5 hours of travel time (for a one way trip).
* Travel compensation, if you are not eligible for a free public transport card; you will receive max. €192,\- net as a monthly allowance.
* Paid holidays per internship term.
* The opportunity to buy Philips products at our Philips shop.

description:
We are looking for an enthusiastic individual who can join our Strategic Intelligence Data Science team within the Strategy \& Corporate Development department, for a minimum of 6 months from August/September 2026 onwards.


**Who we are**


Atradius provides trade credit insurance, surety and collections services worldwide through a strategic presence in 50\+ countries. Its products help protect companies throughout the world from payment risks associated with selling products and services on trade credit.


The Strategy \& Corporate Development (SCD) department supports the Management Board directly through market and portfolio insights, strategic initiatives, and product and service design. The Strategic Intelligence team within SCD is responsible for providing analytical insights as part of our strategy formulation and implementation.


**What will you work on?**


The successful candidate will be part of Strategic Intelligence’s Data Science sub\-team, contributing to the team's analytical activities and supporting the delivery of data\-driven insights to senior stakeholders.


Here are some examples of tasks and responsibilities:


* Contributing to the monthly reporting tasks of the team.
* Maintaining and improving our team’s SQL Server database.
* Automating our reporting processes using SQL and R.
* Analysing and visualising data.
* Undertaking your own analyses to bring fresh insights/ideas to the team.

**Who are we looking for?**


* Holds a BSc or MSc in a quantitative field (e.g. statistics, life/physical sciences, econometrics, data science).
* Fluent in English.
* Strong SQL skills are required. Knowledge of T\-SQL and Microsoft SQL Server would be an advantage.
* Experience with Power BI dashboards is desirable.
* Knowledge of R or another programming language would be a bonus.
* Eligible to live and work in the Netherlands.
* Able to work 60% of the time in the Amsterdam office.

**A great applicant should:**


* Have an inquisitive mindset.
* Be committed to finding better ways to do things.
* Work through problems by themselves, but is also not afraid to ask questions when stuck.
* Have an interest in data, automation, and data visualisation.
* Have solid organisation and communication skills.

**What we offer**


* A friendly and challenging environment to learn: international team (25 colleagues with 10 different nationalities spread over 6 European cities).
* Professional and engaging culture.
* The opportunity and guidance for you to improve your skills and experience.
* An allowance every month.

**Job Summary**

Are you passionate about understanding consumer behavior and turning insights into impactful product innovations?
IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients and health \& biosciences, delivering sustainable innovations that elevate everyday products.


**Scent:** Harnessing the full emotional power of scent, driven by pioneering creativity, science, consumer expertise and a mindful approach to fragrance design


The role is based in Hilversum, be part of a consumer\-focused, analytical, and collaborative team where together we can achieve greatness and make a real impact. Your potential is our inspiration.


The **Consumer Insights (CI) team** plays a key role in the product development process by conducting and analyzing consumer research to understand how to fulfill consumer needs. The team provides insights that support Creative, Marketing, and Sales decision\-making. Using tools such as online consumer connects, preference evaluations, and Home Use Tests (HUTs), the team aims to be the voice of the consumer within IFF.


**We are currently seeking two Consumer Insights Interns to join our Scent team in Hilversum.** This is a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in consumer research within a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients, and health \& biosciences.


**Where You’ll Make a Difference**

* Support the setup, coordination, and execution of consumer research studies
* Conduct desk research to gather relevant consumer and market insights
* Assist in designing questionnaires and test protocols
* Coordinate logistics for consumer studies, including Home Use Tests (HUTs)
* Analyze data from consumer research using statistical and analytical methods
* Supporting the team in preparing presentations summarizing consumer insights and recommendations

description:
Climate risk is becoming increasingly relevant for the planning, design and operation of data centres. Data centres play an important role in the digital economy, but their design standards are often still based on historical weather data. These standards do not always account for future changes in physical climate hazards, such as extreme heat, flooding, drought, water scarcity, storms or other climate\-related stressors.  

At Haskoning, we see climate resilience for data centres as a growing market. We want to strengthen our understanding of the physical climate risks affecting data centres worldwide and further develop our proposition in this field.  

Haskoning has strong expertise in data centre design, climate hazard datasets and physical climate risk assessments. This internship brings these capabilities together.  

During this graduate internship, you will study global physical climate risks for data centres. You will combine technical climate risk analysis with practical engineering knowledge and support the development of Haskoning’s commercial proposition for climate\-resilient data centres. Your work may also contribute to a white paper on this topic.

**What you do as an Intern**
----------------------------


We are looking for a motivated MSc student who is interested in physical climate risks, vulnerability analysis and practical application in an engineering and consultancy environment.
During the internship, you will work across teams and combine climate data, GIS analysis, engineering insights and market\-oriented thinking. You will help assess how different climate hazards may affect data centres in different regions and what this means for design, resilience and adaptation.
The student will work on or support the following activities:
* Using GIS workflows to perform physical climate risk assessments for data centre locations.
* Applying our in\-house Twinn climate hazard datasets and Multi\-Hazard Risk Tool to analyse risks to current and future climate hazards.
* Interviewing data centre design teams to understand asset\-specific vulnerabilities and identifying relevant vulnerability thresholds, impact pathways and potential damage or disruption mechanisms for data centre assets and systems.
* Analysing regional differences in dominant climate hazards and their potential implications for data centre resilience.
* Supporting the development of a Haskoning white paper on global climate risks for data centres.
* Supporting Haskoning’s commercial proposition for climate risk analysis and climate resilience services in the datacentre sector.
* Contributing to a small market study on the potential demand for climate resilience services in this sector.

**Internship: Data \& AI Reporting Analyst (Trade Data)**

We are looking for a highly motivated Data \& AI Reporting Analyst Intern to strengthen our Trade Data team. This role is designed for someone who can work confidently with fragmented, non\-standard data from multiple systems and apply AI, automation, and data science techniques to create accurate reports, insights, and process improvements.  

  



**Your role:**

* Integrate and prepare data from ERP, SAP GTS, TMS, customs brokers, and government systems—often in different formats (Excel, CSV, PDFs, broker templates, EDI extracts).
* Use AI tools to automate data cleaning, pattern detection, reconciliation, and report generation.
* Support development of analytics dashboards and KPI reports using Power BI, Tableau, or Python, etc.based analysis.
* Apply data science thinking to identify trends, inconsistencies, and compliance risks across import/export operations.
* Build and document data pipelines, mapping logic, and automated workflows to support digital customs processes.
* Contribute to automation initiatives focused on improving data accuracy, reducing manual work, and strengthening compliance controls.

**You're the right fit if:**

* Currently studying Data Science, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Supply Chain, International Trade, or similar.
* Strong analytical mindset with hands\-on experience in working with messy, unstructured data.
* Proficiency in AI tools, prompt engineering, and the ability to apply LLMs for data transformation, text extraction, and reporting tasks.
* Foundational knowledge of data science or programming (Power Query, SQL, Python, or R).
* Experience or interest in building dashboards (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
* High attention to detail and an eagerness to learn global customs and trade compliance processes.
* Curiosity and a proactive attitude toward digitalization and automation.

**What You Will Learn:**

* Practical use of AI in compliance, data transformation, and operational automation.
* SAP GTS fundamentals, customs declarations, trade compliance workflows.
* Root\-cause analysis and compliance risk identification using analytics.
* How modern trade compliance teams operate in a data\-driven environment.

**How we work together**

We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office\-based teams, this means working in\-person at least 3 days per week. Onsite roles require full\-time presence in the company’s facilities. Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

description:
About Cordys Analytics
Cordys Analytics is a fast\-growing medtech start\-up and spin\-out from UMC Utrecht.


We develop GALVO, a full\-spectrum AI\-powered cardiology platform that helps healthcare professionals deliver faster, more accurate and more scalable cardiac diagnostics. GALVO supports multiple diagnostic workflows, including resting ECG analysis, Holter monitoring and future applications in remote cardiac monitoring and wearable devices.


Our mission is to improve cardiovascular care by combining artificial intelligence with clinical expertise, enabling earlier diagnosis, better patient outcomes and more efficient healthcare delivery.


As our Holter Operations continue to grow rapidly across Europe, we are looking for an analytical and hands\-on intern or graduate student to help us further professionalise, optimise and scale this critical part of our business.


The assignment
Holter Operations form one of the core services delivered through the GALVO platform in Europe. Holter recordings are received, analysed by trained ECG analysts supported by AI, reviewed through quality control, and returned to healthcare providers as high\-quality clinical reports.


As volumes continue to grow, we want to manage this operation in an increasingly systematic and data\-driven way. To support this, we have developed a Holter Operations Management System: a practical scorecard and capacity management framework that enables us to manage demand, capacity, quality, productivity and financial performance on a weekly basis.


As an intern, you will help further develop, validate and expand this management system, both qualitatively and quantitatively. Rather than writing a report that ends up on a shelf, you will contribute to a management tool that is actively used during our weekly Holter Operations meetings and directly supports operational decision\-making.


Research question
The main research question for this internship or graduation project is:

*How can Cordys Analytics design, measure and manage its Holter Operations so that capacity, quality, turnaround time and profitability remain under control as volumes grow rapidly?*


Possible sub\-questions include:

* How can forecast Holter demand be translated into the required analysis and QC capacity?
* Which KPIs are needed to manage capacity, quality, productivity and financial performance on a weekly basis?
* How can analyst recruitment, onboarding and training be incorporated into capacity planning?
* Which operational buffers are required to prevent backlog growth and turnaround time deterioration?
* How can a practical scorecard be designed that genuinely supports weekly operational decision\-making?

What you will do
You will contribute to a practical management system for a rapidly growing healthcare operation. Typical activities include:

* Mapping the end\-to\-end Holter Operations process, from intake and analysis to QC, reporting and customer delivery.

description:
**About this position**
-----------------------


How can we design electrode materials that convert CO₂ into valuable products efficiently and remain stable during operation?


In this internship, you will investigate copper based catalyst layers and porous electrodes for the electrochemical production of ethylene, an important building block for fuels and chemicals.


You will join TNO’s Materials Solutions team in Eindhoven and work at the interface of materials science, electrochemistry and sustainable technology. Your focus will be on how material composition, microstructure and interfaces influence electrode performance. The assignment combines hands on materials preparation, electrochemical testing, characterisation and scientific analysis.


You will work in a collaborative research environment where questions and new ideas are welcome. We value different perspectives and want everyone to feel able to be themselves at work, regardless of gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, cultural background, disability, neurodivergence, religion or any other aspect of identity.


**What will be your role?**
---------------------------


Your central research question will be: how do the composition, fabrication and structure of copper based electrodes affect CO₂ reduction to ethylene?


Together with your supervisors, you will refine the scope according to the project needs and your learning goals.


Your activities may include:


* Reviewing scientific literature on catalyst layers, ionomers, gas diffusion electrodes and membrane electrode assemblies
* Formulating catalyst inks and fabricating porous electrodes using techniques such as spray coating or doctor blading
* Varying material parameters such as catalyst loading, ionomer content, additives, porosity and layer thickness
* Characterising the resulting materials and electrodes using available microscopy, surface or structural methods
* Assembling membrane electrode assemblies and testing them in electrochemical CO₂ reduction experiments
* Relating material properties and electrode microstructure to selectivity, efficiency and stability
* Analysing data, discussing results with colleagues, and presenting your conclusions and recommendations

By the end of the internship, you will have developed practical experience in electrode fabrication, materials characterisation, electrochemical testing and structure–performance analysis. Your main deliverables will be a documented experimental dataset, a materials design assessment and a final report or thesis.


Throughout your internship, you will collaborate closely with colleagues, discuss your progress, and contribute ideas for improvements and new experiments. You will conclude your internship by delivering a clear report and presenting your key findings.

job_title: Internship | Agentic Decision Support System for 6G RAN
description:
**About this position**
With the evolution of cellular networks, radio access network (RAN) complexity is continuously increasing. The explosive use of AI in recent times, as well as services like extended reality (XR) and the continuous use of video streaming services, are projected to significantly increase the demand for radio resource capacity in 6G networks. Therefore, for network mobile operators, it is critical to be able to monitor and/or forecast spatiotemporal traffic evolutions and take appropriate and timely actions (e.g. deploy additional base stations/antennas, adopt new technological solutions or change the network configuration) to ensure that the network can deliver all services at all times. This graduation project focuses on developing a decision support system (DSS) to autonomously manage the 6G RAN. Thus, the DSS is responsible for performing traffic predictions, predicting potential performance issues, analysing candidate solutions to prevent the predicted performance issues, and provide timely recommendations to the operator on which action to take. The operation of the DSS will be based on the use of a network digital twin (NDT), which will provide the replica of the real network and will allow the evaluation of the various candidate solutions. Moreover, additional automation for the DSS can be achieved by AI agents, which can reason, plan, and take actions. These AI agents can be leveraged for multiple functionalities such as traffic prediction, building/configuring the NDT, recommend candidate solutions for evaluation, and interpret the NDT evaluations.


**What will be your role?**
During this project, a number of key activities will be performed:


* Gain deep understanding of 6G RAN and model candidate solutions addressing capacity issues, such as the deployment of additional macro/small cells, an increased degree of cell sectorization, antenna array upgrades, and spectrum refarming.
* Familiarise yourself with a realistic 6G RAN digital twin, and further enhance the digital twin to support evaluation of the aforementioned candidate solutions.
* Explore the opportunities and challenges of how AI agents can be integrated in the NDT\-based DSS, as well as potentially developing and utilizing AI agents.
* Developing a prototype of the envisioned NDT\-based DSS and use it for demonstration purposes. The prototype should be able to demonstrate the identification of a potential performance/capacity issue, the evaluation of candidate solutions, and the recommendation to the operator.

This graduation project involves closely working with TNO employees and the development of the DSS, which will be re\-used for future work. Further, the project will be conducted as part of the large nationally funded research programme ‘Future Network Services (FNS)’, bringing together more than 60 partners for a targeted period till the middle of 2030\. The proposed project is envisioned to involve collaborations and/or regular interactions w

description:
**About this position**
Are you passionate about catalysis and eager to contribute to a more sustainable chemical industry? At TNO, you will help develop the next generation of flexible, electricity\-driven chemical processes.


As renewable electricity becomes increasingly important in industry, chemical processes must cope with fluctuating operating conditions. To enable this flexibility, catalysts must remain active, selective, and stable under dynamic conditions rather than conventional steady\-state operation.


During this six\-month MSc research internship, you will investigate catalyst behaviour in CO₂ hydrogenation processes and explore strategies to improve catalyst robustness under dynamic operating conditions. You will work within a multidisciplinary team of catalysis, process engineering, and digitalization experts located in Eindhoven and Petten.


This internship offers a unique opportunity to combine fundamental catalysis research with a clear industrial application, contributing to technologies that reduce CO₂ emissions and improve the sustainability of chemical production..


**What will be your role?**
You will contribute to the development of catalyst concepts for flexible and adaptive chemical processes, with a focus on CO₂ hydrogenation.


Your activities will include:


* Designing and performing catalytic experiments under steady\-state and dynamic conditions.
* Investigating catalyst deactivation mechanisms and their impact on activity, selectivity, and stability.
* Operating fixed\-bed reactor systems and analysing reactor performance.
* Characterizing catalysts using techniques such as operando FTIR/DRIFTS, XRD, and SEM.
* Correlating catalyst properties with catalytic performance and deactivation behaviour.
* Developing recommendations for more robust catalysts suitable for fluctuating operating conditions.
* Collaborating with researchers from Eindhoven and Petten working on catalysis, process modelling, and process flexibility.
* Presenting and discussing results within the project team.

This project combines experimental catalysis, advanced characterization, and the energy transition, offering a unique opportunity to contribute to the future of sustainable chemical manufacturing.


**What we expect from you**
We are looking for an enthusiastic MSc student who enjoys experimental research and wants to apply catalysis to real\-world sustainability challenges.


You have:


* An ongoing MSc degree in Chemistry, preferably with a specialization in Inorganic Chemistry or Catalysis.
* A strong interest in heterogeneous catalysis and sustainable chemical processes.
* Affinity with laboratory work and data analysis.
* A proactive mindset and the ability to work independently and in a multidisciplinary team.
* Good communication skills in English.

The following are considered a plus:


* Knowledge of catalyst deactivation and stability.
* Experience with FTIR/DRIFTS, XRD, or related characterization techniques.

job_title: Internship | Impact of Autonomous Transport on International Corridor
description:
**About this position**
Autonomous Transport: a vision for the distant future, or closer than you think? During this internship, you will explore the societal and system\-wide impact of large\-scale deployment of Connected Automated Transport (CAT) on a busy international corridor linking two major European ports. By contributing to this research, you will help shape a more innovative and future\-ready logistics system.


**What will be your role?**
During this internship, you will provide insight into the system\- and societal impact of CAT on the Rotterdam\-Antwerpen corridor. You will analyse success factors and bottlenecks of border crossing CAT and you’ll develop a model to quantify the effects on congestion, emissions and labour demand. You also examine the extent to which the effects are desired or undesired, and what the course of action is to prevent or, conversely, stimulate effects.


During the internship, you are part of a project team specialized in the integration of autonomous transport in logistical operations. You will have the opportunities to share and discuss your ideas within this team.


**What we expect from you**
For this internship (\+/\- 6 months), we are looking for a student with a technical background and affinity with logistics, automotive, vehicle technology or traffic engineering. You have basic experience with python, or are able to get up to speed quickly. You are socially engaged, results driven and able to think outside the box. You combine these qualities with a strong dose of enthusiasm to work on this logistical analysis from both technical and societal perspective. During this internship you are part of the project team and enjoy to work with others and you will learn to work with a lot of autonomy and ownership over your own work. Of course we think it is very valuable if you are an active participant of the team and participate in sharing your knowledge.


**What you'll get in return**
An internship at TNO means working in an environment where substance and impact are central. You will become part of a knowledge organisation where research and practice come together, and where experts collaborate on solutions to current societal and technological challenges.


Your internship is a period in which you can discover what suits you, where your strengths lie and what you would like to learn next. You are part of a professional working environment, gain insight into how things work in practice, and have the opportunity to build experience that goes beyond this internship alone. For many students, an internship is therefore also a first step in discovering whether TNO could be a potential next step after graduation.


In addition, we offer you:


* A professional and innovative internship environment in which you actively contribute to societal and technological challenges, working alongside leading experts in your field.
* Personal and dedicated supervision, with focus on your learning objectives, development

job_title: Internship: Anomaly Detection for Smart Maintenance
description:
We offer you an Ocean of Possibilities . Join our family.


**About us**

Damen aims to become the world's most sustainable and digitally connected shipyard. The Research, Development \& Innovation (RD\&I) department develops and implements the technology and know\-how to achieve these ambitions. We actively assist the business in creating an innovative product portfolio and provide forward\-thinking guidance to improve the quality and performance of Damen's products and services. You will be joining the Data Science team within Damen RD\&I located in Gorinchem. Our department focuses on applying cutting\-edge data and AI solutions to Damen's shipbuilding and maritime operations. The team includes domain experts in physics\-informed machine learning, simulation acceleration, predictive maintenance, computer vision, and operational analytics. This internship is part of Smart Maintenance, a strategic project aimed at using AI to detect abnormal equipment behavior on board vessels before it leads to failure or unplanned downtime.  



**The role**

As an intern, you will work on our Smart Maintenance project where we have developed a n anomaly detection algorithm, which aims to help engineers spot early signs of equipment problems, such as engines, pumps, propulsion and cooling systems, before they escalate into failures. Vessels generate huge amounts of sensor data during operation, and our goal is to turn that data into reliable, trustworthy signals that support maintenance decisions. You will contribute to an existing pipeline that learns what "healthy" equipment behavior looks like and flags deviations from it. Your primary focus will be on a dedicated research topic, to be selected together with the team, that strengthens a specific part of this pipeline — from data selection to detection reliability, health trending, explainability, or deployment. There is room to shape the exact topic based on your interests and background, either before or shortly after you start. This can be a thesis/graduate internship and could start from September onwards.


Possible research topics that we offer, on which the final scope is to be defined together:


* *Model transferability across vessels:* exploring how an anomaly detection model trained on one vessel can be adapted to other vessels, machinery types, or operating environments — including retraining, recalibration, and drift detection strategies.
* *Reliable anomaly detection:* improving detection models to minimize false alarms, adapt to different operating conditions, handle transient events, and quantify prediction confidence.
* *Health and degradation trending:* moving beyond fault detection to identify gradual performance degradation, developing health indicators that give early warning of wear or efficiency loss.
* *Explainable AI and fault diagnosis:* making anomaly models explainable, identifying which sensors or components drive an alert, and supporting root\-cause analysis for engineers.

### **Job Title**


Internship: Global Data Privacy Management### **Job Description**

**Assignment type**: Internship

**Start date**: September 2026

**Assignment** **duration**: 6\-12 months

**Location**: Veghel

**Education** **level**: Bachelor / Master

**Desired** **study**: Law, Business Administration, Information Management Computer Science, or a related field

**Language:** English

**Description of Assignment**
-----------------------------


Join a global Data Privacy team that is building a future\-proof privacy program and help shape how personal data is protected worldwide. Vanderlande is developing a Global Data Privacy Management System to ensure consistent compliance with data protection regulations such as GDPR across all business functions and entities. As an intern, you will support the Data Privacy Team by identifying and documenting data processing activities, validating information with stakeholders, and helping maintain accurate Records of Processing Activities (RoPA). You will also assist with Data Privacy Impact Assessments, vendor assessments, and data subject requests, while contributing to privacy communication materials, training content, templates, and intranet pages.

**Department Description**
--------------------------


As part of our continuous improvement efforts, we are exploring how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can support and optimize engineering processes from sales design to delivery up and to service. Our department is responsible for translating the as sold sales design to a completely engineered baggage handling system, taking into account increasing customer demands. With systems getting larger and more complex, generating the necessary output for project execution is getting more and more complex. This internship aims to identify, evaluate, and prototype AI applications that can bring measurable value to our engineering department.

**Tasks/Responsibilities**
--------------------------

* Support the setup and operationalization of the Data Privacy Management System with a focus on RoPA readiness and data quality.
* Identify and evaluate data processing activities by interviewing process owners and reviewing systems/tools in scope.
* Collect and validate RoPA input (purposes, categories of data/subjects, recipients, retention, security measures, transfers).
* Maintain and update RoPA entries in the selected tooling (or structured Excel/template), ensuring proper versioning and audit trail.
* Identify gaps or risks (e.g., missing lawful basis, unclear retention periods, incomplete processor information) and propose follow‑up actions.
* Support related governance deliverables (e.g., DPIA triggers, vendor privacy checks, documentation standards, reporting).

**Your Profile**
----------------

* Basic programming knowledge (Python preferred)
* Analytical thinking and problem\-solving
* Interest in AI and (mechanical) engineering innovation
* Communication and documentation skills\*Mandatory enrolment

job_title: Internship | AI-based management for sustainability in 6G Cell-Free Networks
description:
**About this position**
Energy savings and sustainability are a big challenge in mobile networks, with the energy consumption continuously increasing due to the ever\-increasing mobile network traffic.
  

Even though efforts are made to reduce the energy consumption of the mobile network in various ways, the radio infrastructure, in particular the base stations, are the components within the whole network to consume the largest amount of energy. A promising new architecture for achieving lower energy consumption while further enhancing the spatial performance fairness compared to traditional cellular networks, is cell\-free networks (CFN).


CFNs consist of multiple simple low\-power access points (APs), which are spatially distributed, and can be seen as a distributed MIMO system. Multiple of these APs can be assigned to serve a single device. The intelligence of the network (for example AP clustering, scheduling, and sleep mode management) is located in a centralized processing unit, which is coordinating and managing the APs. Because networks are designed to handle peak hour traffic, during non\-peak hours some of the APs can go into ‘sleep mode’ in order to reduce the network energy consumption. Hence, sleep mode management (SMM) algorithms are developed to dynamically adapt the ‘mode’ (i.e. active or light/deep sleep) of the APs, according to the traffic load in the network and while still satisfying e.g. coverage and throughput performance requirements. The goal of this project is to deploy deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods for the SMM of the APs. A realistic scenario will be considered for the evaluations, where APs are deployed on lampposts in the city centre of Amsterdam.


**What will be your role?**
During this project, a number of key activities will be performed:


* Gain deep understanding on cell\-free networks, as well as DRL methods.
* Development of a DRL\-based SMM algorithm and integration of the algorithm in an existing simulator of a realistic cell\-free network. The developed algorithm will be evaluated in the simulator in different scenarios and configurations, including testing in different conditions/scenarios than in those trained.
* Implementation of state\-of\-the\-art SMM algorithms from the literature, to be used as baselines for the evaluation of the developed DRL\-based SMM algorithm.
* Derive key insights and conclusions with respect to the performance of the algorithm, as well as the challenges and gains of using DRL compared to heuristic algorithms.

This graduation project involves closely working with TNO employees. Further, the project will be conducted as part of the large nationally funded research programme ‘Future Network Services (FNS)’, bringing together more than 60 partners for a targeted period till the middle of 2030\. The proposed project is envisioned to involve collaborations and/or regular interactions with other FNS partners.


**What we expect from you**
For this graduation project,
